task.title: Create a branch-or-PR readiness JSON command that aggregates local evidence, CI/check inputs, docs-contract status, waiver state, and review disposition.

context.problem_statement: repo-contract-kit has task-worktree readiness, finalizer, docs-impact, changelog, receipt, and context-bundle surfaces, but no single branch-or-PR readiness command that aggregates local evidence and optional hosted-CI/check inputs before humans consider PR update, merge queue, auto-merge, or branch-protection governance. Agents can still over-trust partial green checks or loop across separate commands.
background: AGW-070 added agent-task-ready as a local handoff gate for one prepared task worktree.
AGW-087 added agent-task-finalize to close one task through readiness, lifecycle, task-status, and closeout evidence.
AGW-092 added context bundles with readiness hints but not a final branch/PR decision.
AGW-024 added changelog-update proposal/check support without writing VERSION or CHANGELOG.md.
AGW-099 added local attribution and state-ledger evidence for dirty blockers and task provenance.
GitHub protected branch and status-check docs make hosted status checks and merge queues separate governance layers; this task should aggregate optional evidence but not mutate GitHub state.

repo-contract-kit preflight before packet refresh: git status clean on main ahead of origin through 10e7542; VERSION 0.6.0; AGW-101 focused tests, make test, docs-check, version-check, and git diff --check passed.
agent-workflow-kit source preflight before packet refresh: main ahead of origin through 3aa7deb; backlog-status 6 open, 95 done; next AGW-052; task-status active_task_count 0.
non_goals: Do not merge, approve, label, comment on, enqueue, dequeue, or otherwise mutate GitHub pull requests or branches.
Do not require GitHub credentials or network calls for the default command.
Do not replace agent-task-ready for one prepared task worktree; branch/PR readiness should aggregate or reference that evidence when available.
Do not implement AGW-053 merge-governance examples in this task.
Do not add hosted CI polling loops, background monitors, or auto-merge behavior.
Do not make branch readiness depend on a specific CI provider; use optional input files or explicit flags for hosted check evidence.
Do not weaken docs-impact, receipt, task-status, dirty-state, or version/changelog gates.

report_sources: GitHub protected branches/status checks/merge queue docs searched on 2026-06-16
finalizer_receipt_paths: AGW-101 source closeout commit 3aa7deb completed and selected AGW-052 as next open backlog item.
repo-contract-kit implementation commit 10e7542 is clean and validation passed.
previous_task_state.notes: AGW-052 starts after AGW-101 was implemented, validated, dogfooded, and source-closed. The implementation target is the clean repo-contract-kit source checkout, while this refreshed task packet and later source backlog closeout belong to agent-workflow-kit.

closeout_required_before_start.reason: No active task metadata or checkout dirt is present in the source checkout, and repo-contract-kit validation passed from a clean source checkout at 0.6.0.
closeout_required_before_start.required_next_step: Proceed with one AGW-052 implementation worker in repo-contract-kit. Stop if the design needs GitHub mutation, hosted polling, auto-merge, or branch-protection edits instead of a local no-write readiness surface.
evidence_paths: repo-contract-kit commit 10e7542 validation output captured in parent thread
agent-workflow-kit make agent-next selected AGW-052 with dirty false

goal_alignment.repo_goal: Keep repo-contract-kit as the install-layer guardrail provider for target repositories, exposing deterministic local commands that preserve owner authority before hosted PR or merge governance.

area_contracts.purpose: Stable AI-first CLI where a branch-or-PR readiness command should be exposed in JSON/text form.
area_contracts.source: repo-contract-kit README and existing CLI command parser

area_contracts.purpose: Existing per-task worktree readiness gate; new branch/PR readiness should aggregate or reference it without replacing it.
Docs-contract status and waiver evidence source.
Version/changelog proposal/check evidence source for release-impacting changes.
Installed target-repo Make surface for exposing the readiness command.

stop_conditions: Stop if readiness would mutate GitHub, branches, PRs, labels, comments, merge queues, sidecar state, or target files by default.
Stop if the readiness result can be marked ready while docs-impact, receipt, dirty-state, or declared required checks are missing or failing.
Stop if optional hosted-CI input cannot distinguish unknown, pending, failed, skipped, and successful checks.
Stop if AGW-053 merge-governance examples become necessary to complete AGW-052.

allowed_files: /Volumes/Myrtle/Code/04_Code/repo-contract-kit/scripts/agent_task_ready.py only if a small shared helper is needed
protected_files: GitHub workflows or hosted branch-protection configuration except documentation examples deferred to AGW-053.
User target repositories outside the kit checkout.
Credentials, tokens, API keys, local provider config, or CI secrets.
Existing agent-task-ready per-task semantics except narrow shared helper extraction.
Task lifecycle, closeout, automation-handoff, update-plan, and install behavior unrelated to readiness.
expected_outputs: A no-write CLI command, with JSON and text output, for branch-or-PR readiness. Suggested names are repo_contract_kit.py branch-readiness plus installed make agent-branch-readiness, but the worker may choose a clearer local pattern if documented.

Top-level JSON includes schema_version, command, repo_root, target_ref/base_ref/head_ref or branch metadata, ready boolean, result enum, blockers, warnings, evidence sections, target_repo_writes=false, sidecar_writes=false, and next_safe_commands.

Local evidence sections aggregate git cleanliness, base freshness, docs-impact/waiver state, optional receipt validation/review disposition, optional task readiness/finalizer references, changelog/version check state, and optional CI/check inputs.
Optional CI/check input accepts a local JSON file or explicit CLI values; unknown/missing/pending/failing required checks block readiness unless marked advisory.
Readiness remains local and deterministic by default; network/GitHub API polling is not required and not performed by default.
Installed Make target and README/templates document when this command fits relative to agent-task-ready and AGW-053 merge-governance examples.
Focused tests cover ready, dirty-blocked, docs-missing, invalid receipt/review disposition, failing/pending CI input, unknown optional CI, and no-write behavior.
Version/changelog and source backlog rows close AGW-052 only after validation passes.

acceptance_criteria.description: repo-contract-kit exposes a branch-or-PR readiness command through the AI-first CLI and installed Makefile surface.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Focused CLI/install tests assert command help, JSON output, and installed Make target wiring.

acceptance_criteria.description: Readiness JSON aggregates local evidence into ready/not-ready, blockers, warnings, and next safe commands without target or sidecar writes by default.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Tests assert target_repo_writes=false, sidecar_writes=false, no generated files, and deterministic JSON fields.

acceptance_criteria.description: Docs-impact and waiver state affect readiness: missing required docs without waiver blocks, covered docs pass, explicit no-docs-needed is recorded.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Focused tests with changed files and no-docs-needed inputs.

acceptance_criteria.description: Optional CI/check input can mark required checks success, pending, failed, skipped, or unknown; pending/failing/missing required checks block readiness.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Focused tests using local JSON check input or equivalent CLI flags.

acceptance_criteria.description: Receipt/review disposition input is validated when provided and can block when evidence is invalid or failing.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Focused tests using valid and invalid receipt/review evidence.

acceptance_criteria.description: The command does not merge, approve, comment, call GitHub, enqueue merge queues, or edit branch-protection settings.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Diff inspection and no-write/no-network tests or documented no-network design.

acceptance_criteria.description: Docs explain the command's relationship to agent-task-ready, finalizer, branch protection, status checks, and AGW-053 merge-governance examples.
Version/changelog and source backlog rows close AGW-052 after validation.
acceptance_criteria.verification: make version-check in kit; backlog-check/backlog-split-check in source.

commands.command: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 -m unittest tests.test_repo_contract_kit_cli tests.test_install tests.test_agent_task_ready
commands.expected_result: pass; add a focused test module if the implementation creates one
pass in agent-workflow-kit after source closeout
pass in agent-workflow-kit after source closeout
pass in agent-workflow-kit after source closeout

evidence_to_capture: repo-contract-kit focused and full test output
sample readiness JSON for a ready branch and a blocked branch
final clean status for both checkouts

readiness_check.command: Use the newly added branch/PR readiness command on the AGW-052 branch if available, otherwise run existing make test/docs/version gates and record why per-task agent-task-ready is not applicable to the source checkout.
readiness_check.expected_result: readiness passes or explicit unavailable/omitted rationale with compensating validation evidence

lifecycle_action.command: No installed task metadata is expected in the kit source checkout; record final validation and close worker after commit/push.
lifecycle_action.expected_result: implementation committed and pushed, or blocker recorded

final_task_status.expected_result: no active AGW-052 source task remains, or any remaining task metadata is explicitly explained

closeout_preview.command: Use repo-contract-kit closeout preview only if task metadata/worktrees were prepared; otherwise record not applicable for direct source work.
closeout_preview.expected_result: preview reports no unsafe cleanup, or unavailable/omitted rationale recorded

closeout_requirements.dirty_state_explanation: Final handoff must say whether both checkouts are clean, only expected files remain dirty, cleanup is blocked, or unrelated dirt was preserved.

docs_impact.notes: This adds a user-facing CLI/Make readiness surface and should update kit docs/templates plus changelog/version.

known_risks: Readiness can be mistaken for merge permission if docs do not separate local evidence from hosted governance.
Optional CI/check inputs can create false readiness if unknown or stale data is treated as passing.
Duplicating agent-task-ready logic can drift from the per-task gate.
A command that shells out to many checks can become slow or non-deterministic if not scoped.
stop_conditions: Implementation needs GitHub credentials, network polling, PR mutation, merge queue operations, or branch protection edits.
Readiness output lacks explicit blockers/warnings and no-write metadata.
Required docs-impact or receipt evidence can be missing while ready remains true.
Full kit tests or source backlog checks fail.

approval.notes: Approved as part of serial workflow-stack backlog execution; AGW-052 is the next selected open item after AGW-101.

overlap_warnings: Use exactly one AGW-052 implementation worker.
The worker owns repo-contract-kit implementation, tests, docs, version, and changelog.
The parent owns the source task-packet commit and final source backlog closeout review.
Do not start AGW-053 merge-governance examples until AGW-052 readiness command is complete.

handoff.owner: one repo-contract-kit implementation worker; parent for source packet/backlog integration
handoff.next_packet_hint: AGW-053 should add hosted merge-governance examples after AGW-052 ships the local readiness contract.
